rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GEORGE MATRANGA TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Approval Of Compromise Of Claim Or Action Or Disposition Of Proceeds Of Judgment For Minor Or Person With A Disability
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Please review Local Rule 14.1 D. for information regarding remote appearances. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Court to determine how to proceed. At the 02/15/2022 hearing the court stated it wants to see in the amended petition what the statutory authority for the petition is, what the relevant legal standard is, what the material terms are of the settlement and what the parties think the effect of the court approving this settlement is. It appears none of this information requested by the court was included in the 5/12/2022 Supplement. 2) At the November 16, 2021 hearing the judicial officer indicated the minor should not have to wait until they are 30 for distributions. The amended settlement requests their distribution be put into a blocked account until 18 then held by the trustee until the minor reaches the age of 25. 3) The settlement language requests the transfers of real property be deemed to be as of September 27, 2018 (date of death of George John Matranga). 4) Settlement requests enforcement under CCP 664.6. 5) The proposed order to approve settlement says attached as Exhibit A is a copy of the amended settlement. No such attachment was included in the revised proposed order.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN RIGNEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/11/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Examiner received a double sided copy of the 5/12/2022 Declaration of Travis Neal. Please provide the required single sided courtesy copy. [See LRSF 14.7.] There may be additional issues when the required single sided courtesy copy is received and have been reviewed. 2) Please provide the required single sided courtesy copy of the 8/17/2022 and 8/23/2022 filings. [See LRSF 14.7] There may be additional issues when the required courtesy copies are received and have been reviewed. 3) Please explain and file legal basis/cite probate code sections for hiring and paying Miller Starr Regalia without court authority. 4) Please file current vesting deeds for all the real property. 5) Please file proof of satisfaction and release of the creditor claims signed by the creditors. 6) Please file timesheets for attorney ordinary statutory services so that the Court may determine whether extraordinary fees are "just and reasonable" (Prob. C. 10811(a)). 7) The request for distribution on page 14 paragraph 9 is not sufficient. The petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed to each distributee, including the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) to be distributed to each beneficiary. [See LRSF 14.35 I. 6)] 8) Please explain the transfer of each firearm and state who will receive each firearm. Please explain who is safeguarding the firearms and if they have a valid license and gun safe. Please provide a memo of points and authorities regarding transfer of the firearms. 9) Please file proof the 12/21/2021 trusts costs of $121.03 has been repaid to the estate. 10) On Disbursements please explain and provide the legal basis explain the Reimbursement to of retainer payment to Hartog, Baer & hand and why this $1,500.00 hasn't been deduced from the requested attorney fees. 11) On disbursements please explain and life a receipt for unknown roof repair for $595.00. 12) The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. 13) As the account is 9 months out of date, please also provide account statements from August 2022. 14) Please explain why over $250,000.00 has been kept in a single account over the $250,000.00 FDIC limit. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/26/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JEWEL DAVIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by the judicial officer. This petition is ordered off calendar. Status on filing a petition to admit the lost will is set for 10/4/2022. Petitioner seeks distribution pursuant to a lost will when no will has been admitted to probate. Petitioner is ordered to file a petition to admit the lost will and to address the presumption of revocation in Probate Code 6124. Petitioner must lodge a copy of the lost will as if it were an original will, republish, and notice all heirs including the great-grandchildren that did not receive notice of the original petition (Probate Code 1202). Please also provide evidence regarding the circumstances under which the purported lost will was discovered including but not limited to the date the purported lost will was discovered.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRANCIS GREGORY METCALF
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The document sought to be admitted to probate as a valid will is a notepad on which Lurline O'Brien, one of the decedent's sisters, handwrote the terms dictated by the decedent in his presence. The decedent then dated and signed the document prepared by Lurline O'Brien. However, the document was not signed by two witnesses in accordance with Probate Code section 6110(c)(1). Section 6110(c)(2) provides that: "If a will was not executed in compliance with paragraph (1), the will shall be treated as if it was executed in compliance with that paragraph if the proponent of the will establishes by clear and convincing evidence that, at the time the testator signed the will, the testator intended the will to constitute the testator's will." 2) The document sought to be admitted to probate as a valid undated codicil is a copy of the notepad sought to be admitted as a valid will. The decedent scratched out the provision making a $10,000 cash gift to Sam St. Germain, one of the decedent's nephews, with a handwritten notation "deceased", and signed the copy. However, the original notepad was kept intact. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.
